返回本批次

比亚迪Seal 08首发刀片电池2.0：续航里程1000公里，充电5分钟，684马力

BYD Seal 08 debuts with Blade Battery 2.0: 1,000km range, 5-min charging, 684hp

electrek.co 作者 breve 分数 48 评论 46 2026/04/28 14:32
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 比亚迪在本周末的北京车展上推出了其全新旗舰电动轿车Seal 08。该车配备比亚迪第二代刀片电池，CLTC续航里程超过1,000公里，兆瓦级闪充可在5分钟内增加400公里续航里程。
  2. 这标志着刀片电池2.0和闪充组合首次登陆全尺寸轿车，将其定位为高端电动轿车的直接挑战者。
  3. 刀片电池2.0和闪充
  4. Seal 08建立在800伏高压平台上，配备比亚迪第二代刀片电池，该电池采用磷酸铁锂（LFP）化学物质。比亚迪于 3 月初首次发布刀片电池 2.0，声称在 CLTC 条件下的续航里程超过 1,000 公里，大约是第一代刀片电池的两倍。
  5. 闪存充电功能是主要功能。比亚迪声称该系统可以在五分钟内补充 400 公里的续航里程，并在同一时间内从 10% 充电到 70%。在低至 -30°C 的寒冷天气下，充电 20% 至 97% 需要 12 分钟。

Original Summary

  1. BYD unveiled the Seal 08, its new flagship electric sedan, at the Beijing Auto Show this weekend. The vehicle packs BYD’s second-generation Blade Battery targeting over 1,000 km of CLTC range and megawatt-level flash charging that adds 400 km of range in five minutes.
  2. It marks the first time the Blade Battery 2.0 and flash charging combination land in a full-size sedan, positioning it as a direct challenger to higher-end electric sedans.
  3. Blade Battery 2.0 and flash charging
  4. The Seal 08 is built on an 800-volt high-voltage platform and equipped with BYD’s second-generation Blade Battery, which uses lithium iron phosphate (LFP) chemistry. BYD first announced the Blade Battery 2.0 in early March, claiming over 1,000 km of range under CLTC conditions — roughly double what the first-generation Blade Battery delivered.
  5. The flash charging capability is the headline feature. BYD claims the system can replenish 400 km of range in five minutes and charge from 10% to 70% in the same timeframe. In cold weather down to -30°C, a 20% to 97% charge takes 12 minutes.

评论（默认中文）

ZeroGravitas 2026/04/28 16:45
本周将举办一场大型中国车展，因此会发布很多公告。

同一网站有一个概述帖子：

https://electrek.co/2026/04/26/beijing-auto-show-2026-insane...

> 在展会的一个大厅中，展出的电动汽车车型比整个美国的可用车型还要多。本次展会共有17个展厅。十七。它们的电动汽车数量都比美国市场多。

> 此次车展共有 1,451 辆汽车，其中包括 181 辆全球首发车和 71 辆概念车，在两个场馆的展览面积达到创纪录的 380,000 平方米。它现在是世界上最大的车展——而且距离还很遥远。

There's a big Chinese auto show happening this week so there's a lot of announcements.

The same site has an overview post:

https://electrek.co/2026/04/26/beijing-auto-show-2026-insane...

> In a single hall at the show, there were more EV models on display than there are available ones in the entire United States. There are 17 halls at this show. Seventeen. And they all have more EVs than the US market.

> The show features 1,451 vehicles, including 181 world premieres and 71 concept cars, sprawling across a record-breaking 380,000 square meters of exhibition space at two venues. It’s now the largest auto show in the world — and it’s not even close.

在 HN 中查看
sigmoid10 2026/04/28 17:02
这里没有什么惊喜。无论从产量还是销量来看，中国已经是最大的汽车市场。而且差距很大。美国和欧盟的汽车工业正在被抛在后面。强大的工业基础和不完全落入垂死的化石工业口袋的独裁政权似乎是本世纪的胜利组合。如果有什么阻碍他们的话，那就是独生子女政策的长期后果，该政策加剧了发达国家常见的人口问题。
No surprises here. China is already the biggest car market, both by production and units sold. And the gap is substantial. The US and EU automotive industries are getting left in the dust. A powerful industrial base and an authoritarian regime that is not completely in the dying fossil industry's pocket seem to be a winning combination this century. If anything stops them, it will be the long term consequences of the one child policy, which turbocharged the usual developed country population issues.
在 HN 中查看
khriss 2026/04/28 16:55
中国似乎要么正在将美国抛在后面，要么在未来的每一项技术上都处于惊人的距离。

回顾过去，我想知道我们是否会认为这一时期与 1957 年苏伊士运河危机对英国造成的影响相似。

编辑：拼写

China seems to be either leaving the US behind, or within striking distance in every single technology for the future.

Looking back, I wonder if we will see this period as similar to what the 1957 Suez crisis did to the UK.

Edit: Spelling

在 HN 中查看
cjs_ac 2026/04/28 17:06
5 分钟内 400 公里的数字依赖于找到能够持续提供 1 MW 功率的充电器；一位著名的英国 YouTuber 刚刚表示，在他测试电动汽车的这些年里，他无法找到能够持续超过 100 kW 的公共充电器。

尽管我认为电动汽车是未来——我的下一辆车也将是未来——但在它们达到其宣传的潜力之前，还有很多基础设施需要到位和改进，就像石油动力汽车一样。

The 400 km in 5 minutes figure relies on finding a charger that can consistently deliver 1 MW; a prominent UK YouTuber just reported being unable to find a public charger that can consistently exceed 100 kW in all the years he's been testing electric cars.

As much as I think that electric cars are the future - and my next car will be one - there's a lot of infrastructure that needs to be put in place and improved before they can reach their advertised potential, just as there was for petroleum-powered cars.

在 HN 中查看
xdex 2026/04/28 17:21
我毫不怀疑这在英国和欧洲是正确的，但这是一家在中国汽车博览会上的中国公司，所以我想这些充电器会在中国各地出现。

也许有一天世界其他地方会迎头赶上？

I don’t doubt that’s true in the UK and Europe but this is a Chinese company at a Chinese car expo so I’d imagine that these chargers are popping up all over China.

Maybe one day the rest of the world will catch up?

在 HN 中查看
bjohnson225 2026/04/28 17:13
在这一点上，电动汽车经济学似乎将使欧盟政府的命令变得无关紧要。电动汽车的购买成本和运行成本将更便宜。唯一剩下的问题是快速充电基础设施的部署速度有多快，这将使电动汽车成为默认选择，即使对于我们这些无法在家充电的人来说也是如此。
At this point it seems EV economics will make the EU government mandates irrelevant. Electric cars will be cheaper to buy and cheaper to run. The only remaining question is how quickly rapid charging infra will be deployed which will make electric the default choice even for those of us who cannot charge at home.

spwa4 2026/04/28 16:33
（每千瓦时 0.33 至 0.35 欧元，旧合同为 0.4 欧元，是法国或美国价格的两倍，比传说中昂贵的瑞士还要高。顺便说一句，仍然比相同范围的天然气便宜得多）
(0.33 to .35 euro per kwh, .4 on an old contract, double the price in France or US, and more than legendarily expensive Switzerland. Still way cheaper than the same range in gas btw)

mschild 2026/04/28 17:01
如果在家充电。

如果将快速充电器或大量商业产品纳入其中，您将看到每千瓦时 0.6 的电量。别介意很多公司正在做的订阅/帐户废话。

不管怎样，当我买一辆新车时，我仍然只会购买电动汽车。

If charging at home.

Bring in fast chargers or a lot of the commercial offerings into the mix and you're looking at .6 per kWh. Never mind the subscription/account bullshit a lot of companies are doing.

Regardless of that, I would still only ever buy an EV when I get a new car.

Both maintenance and charging are negligible costs.

a96 2026/04/28 16:46
是的。即使有政府提供的可笑补贴和支持，ICE 的供给和维护仍然是一场噩梦。 （而且效率低下且污染严重。）
Yes. Even with the ludicrous subsidies and support from governments, ICEs are a nightmare to feed and maintain. (And inefficient and massively polluting.)
在 HN 中查看
SideburnsOfDoom 2026/04/28 17:04
父帖子中的“能源和维护成本”之前有“多个”一词

这意味着内燃机汽车的能源和维护成本是电动汽车能源和维护成本的倍数（即几倍）。

因此，电动汽车能源和维护成本只是内燃机能源和维护成本的一小部分。

如果你愿意的话，你可以辩论这个断言，但首先你必须成功地阅读它。

There are the words "a multiple of" in the parent post, just before the words "energy and maintenance costs"

Which means that ICE Vehicle energy and maintenance costs are a multiple of (i.e. several times that of) EV energy and maintenance costs.

And so EV energy and maintenance costs are a fraction of the ICE energy and maintenance costs.

You can debate this assertion if you like, but first you have to read it successfully.

在 HN 中查看
swiftcoder 2026/04/28 16:45
我迫不及待地想把同样的电池装进一辆低端汽车里。真的很想在这几天看到一款 3 万欧元范围内的 >750 公里的竞争者
I can't wait till they stuff the same battery in a down-market car. Would really like to see a >750km contender in the €30k range one of these days
在 HN 中查看
maxglute 2026/04/28 17:01
真正的消息是比亚迪正在制造blade2，并且未来将推出闪充（10m - 97%）标准，即所有价位的所有车型的充电速度将与加油箱一样快。据推测，闪充网络也向所有兼容车辆开放（可能是对旧电池的某种滴定）。一旦blade2激增，闪充吞吐量=可以将许多小批量（即便利店）转变为加油站等充电站。我认为闪存充电基础设施基本上只是从现有的电力基础设施中使用大量刀片2电池进行滴注充电，因此不需要对电网进行重大检修=几乎任何地方的盒内充电基础设施（即，如果电网支持重型工业交流电，它可以支持闪存柜）将与充电时间一样长。

E：@10m 充电，系统基本上扩展到典型的油箱加气交易时间，即 10-12m。电池存储的大小可以承受高峰时段的吞吐量，然后在停顿期间通过电网或屋顶太阳能充电。基本上与天然气基础设施持平。该计划还包括对旧电池进行二次利用，即 60-70% 的 Blade1……许多第一代蝙蝠即将退役，通过升级改造，存储基本上将“免费”。中国最近强制实施了又名全电池循环经济。

The real news is BYD is making blade2, and flash charging (10m - 97%) standard going forward, i.e. all models all price points will recharge as fast as filling gas tank. The flash charging network supposedly also open to all compatible vehicles (probably some sort of titration on older batteries). Once blade2 proliferate, flash charging throughput = can convert many small lots, i.e. convenience stores into recharge stations like gas stations. I think flash charging infra basically just has a fuckload of blade2 batteries drip charging from existing electric infra, so no need for major grid overhaul = the station in box charging infra almost anywhere (i.e. if grid supports heavy industrial AC, it can support flash cabinets) is going to be as big as charging time.

E: @10m charge, the system basically is scaled to typical gas tank up transaction times, i.e. 10-12m. The battery storage sized to survive rush hour throughput then charge off grid or roof solar during lull. Basically parity with gas infra. The plan is also to second life old batteries, i.e. 60-70% blade1s... lots of 1st gen bats retiring, storage is going to be essentially "free" via upcycling. AKA entire battery circular economy PRC mandated recently.

在 HN 中查看
close04 2026/04/28 17:14
闪充为1MW-1000V、1000A。峰值功率由电网或某些储能装置提供，可以及时补充以供下一辆车使用。我们需要廉价且微型的电池，以便能够在 MW/MWh 范围内安装如此多的存储和峰值功率输出，以便放置在各处。对于 1MW/3MWh 电池等目前的容器尺寸，掩埋并不总是一种选择。或者，为什么不呢，也许我们需要更少的汽车。
Flash charging is 1MW - 1000V, 1000A. That peak power is provided by the grid or some energy storage that can be replenished in time for the next cars. We need cheap and tiny batteries to be able to install that much storage and peak power output in the MW/MWh range to put all over the place. And at current container size for something like a 1MW/3MWh battery, burying is not always an option. Or, why not, maybe we need fewer cars.
在 HN 中查看
ranguna 2026/04/28 16:23
在欧洲要花多少钱？他们是如何设法将如此多的能量装入电池组的？
How much will it cost in Europe and how did they manage to fit so much energy into their battery pack?
在 HN 中查看
pjc50 2026/04/28 16:32
后一个问题：它是 92kWh，即使是一些入门级汽车售价的两倍，这也不是没有道理的。

深入了解包装： https://www.batterydesign.net/byd-blade-2-0-compared-to-1-0/ ;他们似乎做了一些很好的老式机械工程，以最大限度地减少电池的“非电池”部分，同时保持液体冷却有效。

欧洲的成本：基于过去的汽车..可能多 50-100%？更高的税收和更高的利润。

我可以找到以前的 Seal 高级规格版本（390kW / 83kWh），价格为 46k 英镑： https://www.arnoldclark.com/new-cars/byd/seal/390kw-excellen... ，或者您也可以以 321 英镑的价格租赁。英国租赁似乎是最后一个可能获得实际优于市场交易的地方，这很奇怪。

Latter question: it's 92kWh, which is not unreasonable even if it's twice what some entry level cars are being sold with.

Deep dive on the pack: https://www.batterydesign.net/byd-blade-2-0-compared-to-1-0/ ; it seems they've done some good old fashioned mechanical engineering to minimize the "not cells" part of the battery while keeping the liquid cooling effective.

Cost in Europe: based on past cars .. maybe 50-100% more? Higher taxes AND higher margins.

I can find the previous Seal at £46k for the premium spec version (390kW / 83kWh): https://www.arnoldclark.com/new-cars/byd/seal/390kw-excellen... , or you can lease it for £321. UK leasing seems to be the last place it's possible to get an actual beat the market deal, which is odd.

as usual double to triple the Chinese price for EVs

aurareturn 2026/04/28 15:28
续航里程 1,000 公里、功率 684 马力，售价 42,000 美元。这真是一笔好买卖。
$42,000 for 1,000KM of range and 684hp. That's just ridiculously a good deal.
在 HN 中查看
zelos 2026/04/28 16:41
出于政治原因，我一直避开中国电动汽车，但这变得越来越困难。我的起亚 EV6 的租约在 6 个月后结束后，我可以升级到续航里程增加 10% 的新车型，或者改用欧洲品牌之一，这些品牌的充电速度大多只有一半左右。

梅赛德斯 CLA 似乎是唯一一款性能明显更好但价格又不那么疯狂的车型。

I've avoided Chinese EVs for political reasons, but it's getting harder. After the lease on my Kia EV6 ends in 6 months I can either upgrade to the new model with a whole 10% more range, or switch to one of the European brands which mostly have around half the charging speed.

The Mercedes CLA seems to be the only model that's significantly better and not totally crazy money.

a96 2026/04/28 16:49

Yep. Even the previous Seal was a very tempting option. If only it wasn't a privacy game over. I've also eyed the CLA, but it seems over the top. Too bad KiaHyundai is mostly doing van sized things.

kilroy123 2026/04/28 16:36
我相信埃隆正在让 SpaceX 上市，因为他知道特斯拉已经熟了。中国人已经赢了。他们生产的汽车售价为 10,000 美元，续航里程为 1,000 公里，规模令人瞠目结舌。

他知道特斯拉已经时日无多了。

I believe Elon is taking SpaceX public because he knows that Tesla is cooked. The Chinese have already won. They're pumping out cars for $10,000, 1,000 km range, and at jaw-dropping scale.

He knows Tesla is on borrowed time.

在 HN 中查看
pjc50 2026/04/28 16:43
特斯拉的处境很奇怪：人们不断指出其股价与汽车业务脱钩，但该股却拒绝下跌。部分原因是他们仍在兜售完整 FSD 的梦想。

中国企业似乎正在无视FSD，直接冲向电动汽车的王冠。日本公司坚持使用混合动力汽车，他们在这方面做得很好，但仍然依赖汽油。

美国根本不会让中国汽车进入，而是会继续购买10万美元的耗油卡车，因为这就是市场的需求。

Tesla are in a strange place: people keep pointing out how decoupled the stock price is from the car business, and the stock refuses to go down. Partly because they're still selling the dream of full FSD.

Chinese companies seem to be ignoring FSD and going straight for the full EV crown. Japanese companies are clinging to hybrids, which they do well, but are still dependent on petrol.

America simply won't let the Chinese cars in and will continue to buy $100k gas guzzler trucks, because that's what the market demands.

在 HN 中查看
londons_explore 2026/04/28 16:55
在美国，制造业总是暂时的。 当你所有的工人都想获得高薪时，你就无法在制造成本敏感的产品方面获胜。

应该走苹果路线——在美国设计，在中国制造，并使用围墙花园来确保硬件克隆不受欢迎并且无法运行您的任何应用程序/功能。

Manufacturing was always going to be temporary in the USA. You just can't win at manufacturing cost sensitive goods when all your workers want to be highly paid.

Should have gone the Apple route - design in the USA, make in China, and use a walled garden to ensure hardware clones aren't desirable and can't run any of your apps/features.

在 HN 中查看
ZeroGravitas 2026/04/28 16:59
特斯拉一半以上的汽车在中国生产。人们普遍认为它们的质量比美国制造的更好。
Tesla makes more than half its cars in China. They are generally regarded as better in quality than the US built ones.

nhinck2 2026/04/28 15:51

pjc50 2026/04/28 16:24

I don't see why not, it's all contributing to acceleration, although it's going to be rough on the tyres.
在 HN 中查看
swiftcoder 2026/04/28 16:42
嘿，即使是相当普通的电动汽车对轮胎来说也很粗糙，所以如果他们正在寻找运动加速，没有人会介意这种权衡
And hey, even fairly modest EVs are rough on tyres, so no one is going to mind that tradeoff if they are looking for sporty acceleration
在 HN 中查看
cyberax 2026/04/28 16:57
我认为轮胎问题并不是什么大问题。电动汽车使用同步电机和牵引力控制，以避免正常行驶过程中由于扭矩不均匀而造成的额外磨损。
I think the tyre problem is not really a thing. EVs use synchronized motors and traction control to avoid extra wear due to uneven torque during normal driving.
在 HN 中查看
Mashimo 2026/04/28 17:08
我不记得是在这里还是在 Reddit 上，但我从一家轮胎店/机械师那里读到，一些电动汽车用户经常更换轮胎，因为电动汽车很容易驾驶得非常激进。
I can't remember if it was here or on reddit, but I read from a tyre shop / mechanic, that some EV users replace their tyres very often, because EV cars make it easy to drive very aggressive.
在 HN 中查看
swiftcoder 2026/04/28 17:11
有趣的是，我的起亚 Niro EV 的轮胎磨损速度比家族中的两辆同等内燃机汽车快得多。

也就是说，Niro 的重量比其他车辆重约 50%，并且它的加速/制动性能明显更高，因此我担心它的平均驾驶难度会更大。

Anecdotally, my Kia Niro EV goes through tyres a lot faster than the two equivalent internal combustion vehicles in the family.

That said, the Niro weighs ~50% more than the other vehicles, and it has significantly higher acceleration/braking, so I'd hazard it gets driven harder on average.

在 HN 中查看
mschild 2026/04/28 17:04
由于电池而增加的重量是轮胎磨损的更大问题。许多电动汽车的重量比内燃机汽车重 500 公斤。
The increased weight due to the battery is the bigger issue for wear on tires. A lot of EVs weigh a good 500kg more than their ICE counterparts.
在 HN 中查看
Ekaros 2026/04/28 17:07
我认为更大的问题是扭矩。电动汽车具有更大的扭矩，并且更易于使用，因此它们会更频繁地打滑，从而导致磨损。
I think bigger issue is torque. EVs have lot more torque and it is easier to use, so they can slip more often which then leads to wear.
在 HN 中查看
metalman 2026/04/28 16:48
旗舰杀手。
Flagship killer.
在 HN 中查看
askl 2026/04/28 16:37
很高兴看到电动汽车市场的创新。

与此同时，传统电动汽车制造商特斯拉除了愚蠢的玩具项目之外什么也不做。 （或者更确切地说，大肆宣传愚蠢的玩具项目，但实际上什么也没做）

Nice to see innovation in the EV market.

Meanwhile legacy EV maker Tesla continues doing nothing other than silly toy projects. (Or rather hyping up silly toy projects and actually doing nothing at all)

在 HN 中查看
himata4113 2026/04/28 16:36
那不是电池，那是可重复使用的炸弹。好在他们也想出了如何防止他们出现失控的反应。
That's not a battery, that's a reusable bomb. Good thing they also figured out how to keep them from having runaway reactions.
在 HN 中查看
adrian_b 2026/04/28 17:20
电池是比油箱威力小得多的炸弹，因为它们不能产生如此大量的气体。电解质的分解可能会产生气体，但电池中的电解质比燃料箱中的燃料少得多，并且燃烧的燃料吸收了空气中的氧气，增加了热膨胀气体的体积。

电池的危险主要在于难以扑灭的火源。例如，用水灭火实际上可能会因水分解产生的气体而引起爆炸。

Batteries are much less powerful bombs than fuel tanks, because they cannot produce a so great volume of gas. The decomposition of the electrolyte may produce gas, but there is much less electrolyte in a battery than fuel in a fuel tank, and burning fuel incorporates oxygen from the air, increasing the volume of the hot expanding gas.

Batteries are dangerous mainly as sources of fire that is difficult to extinguish. For instance extinguishing with water may actually cause an explosion, by gas produced by the decomposition of water.

在 HN 中查看
sigmoid10 2026/04/28 16:50
这只是一块92kWh的电池。市场上已经有很多100kWh或以上的汽车。这只是每升汽油约 10kWh 的平均燃料箱中存储的能量的一小部分。内燃机汽车只是将大部分能量损失为热量 实际的 爆炸。但在极端事故中，普通汽车甚至比续航里程最长的电动汽车还要大得多。
It's just a 92kWh battery. There are many cars with 100kWh or more on the market already. And that's only a fraction of the energy stored in an average fuel tank at ~10kWh per litre of gas. A combustion car just loses most of that energy to heat from actual explosions. But in an extreme crash, a normal car is a much bigger bomb than even the longest range EV.
在 HN 中查看
